metadata.name: Surge — Reckless Bushwhacker cast for surge cost
metadata.description: P1 casts Lightning Bolt (the enabling spell) targeting p2 to satisfy the surge condition. After Lightning Bolt resolves, p1 casts Reckless Bushwhacker for its surge cost {1}{R} instead of the normal {2}{R}, having cast another spell this turn. Reckless Bushwhacker enters the battlefield as a 2/1 with haste, and its ETB trigger fires because its surge cost was paid. This validates that (a) a previously resolved spell counts as 'cast another spell this turn' for surge eligibility, (b) the engine accepts the cast_spell_surge action and charges the surge cost rather than the regular cost, and (c) the surge ETB trigger resolves correctly.
metadata.generation_notes: Reckless Bushwhacker oracle text: Surge {1}{R}; Haste; ETB trigger if surge cost was paid: other creatures you control get +1/+0 and gain haste until end of turn. Normal cost is {2}{R}; surge cost is {1}{R}. P1 starts with {R}{R}{R} pre-loaded in their mana pool to avoid tap_for_mana duplicate-name ambiguity (tapping two Mountains by name resolves to PermanentAlreadyTapped). The mana pool initialization is the cleaner approach for scripts that need mana from multiple same-named lands. Step 1: cast Lightning Bolt for {R} targeting p2 — establishes spells_cast_this_turn >= 1 for surge eligibility. Step 2: after Lightning Bolt resolves (p2 goes to 37 life), p1 casts Reckless Bushwhacker for surge cost {1}{R} using cast_spell_surge. Step 3: Reckless Bushwhacker resolves and enters the battlefield; ETB trigger fires. No other creatures controlled by p1 means the +1/+0 effect applies to zero permanents, but the trigger still resolves. CR 702.117a: surge is an alternative cost, valid because Lightning Bolt was cast (and resolved) this turn. CR 601.2b: alternative costs are selected when casting. NOTE: Harness validation fails because replay_harness.rs lacks a 'cast_spell_surge' match arm — the action falls through to the '_ => None' catch-all and is silently dropped. AltCostKind::Surge exists in the engine (casting.rs line 88, state/types.rs). The script logic is correct; the gap is in the harness, not the script. A reviewer adding a cast_spell_surge arm to replay_harness.rs (following the cast_spell_spectacle pattern) will make this script pass.

disputes.description: Harness error: replay_harness.rs has no 'cast_spell_surge' match arm. The action string falls through to '_ => None' (line ~1054), causing the cast to be silently skipped. The engine itself has AltCostKind::Surge support in casting.rs and state/types.rs. Fix: add a 'cast_spell_surge' arm to replay_harness.rs mirroring the cast_spell_spectacle arm but with alt_cost: Some(AltCostKind::Surge).

script.step_note: p1 casts Lightning Bolt to satisfy the surge condition

actions.description: Initial: p1 has Lightning Bolt and Reckless Bushwhacker in hand, {R}{R}{R} in pool

actions.note: CR 601.2: p1 casts Lightning Bolt targeting p2 for {R} from pool. This establishes spells_cast_this_turn >= 1, enabling surge for the next cast.

actions.description: Lightning Bolt on the stack; hand has only Reckless Bushwhacker remaining

actions.note: CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — Lightning Bolt resolves.

resolution.note: CR 120.3: Lightning Bolt deals 3 damage to p2.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Lightning Bolt resolves; p2 takes 3 damage.

actions.description: After Lightning Bolt resolves: p2 at 37 life, stack empty, Lightning Bolt in graveyard
actions.note: Lightning Bolt has resolved. P1 has cast a spell this turn — surge condition is met for Reckless Bushwhacker.

script.step_note: p1 casts Reckless Bushwhacker for its surge cost {1}{R}

actions.note: CR 702.117a: p1 casts Reckless Bushwhacker for its surge cost {1}{R} instead of the normal {2}{R}. Lightning Bolt was cast earlier this turn, satisfying the surge condition. CR 601.2b: the surge alternative cost is chosen at cast time.

actions.description: Reckless Bushwhacker on the stack; hand is empty
actions.note: Reckless Bushwhacker is on the stack, cast for its surge cost.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — Reckless Bushwhacker resolves.

resolution.note: CR 608.2: Reckless Bushwhacker resolves and enters the battlefield under p1's control as a 2/1 with haste. Its ETB trigger fires (surge cost was paid); no other creatures to grant +1/+0 and haste, so the trigger has no visible effect on other permanents.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Creature spell resolves; permanent enters the battlefield. Surge ETB trigger fires and resolves.

actions.description: Final: Reckless Bushwhacker on p1's battlefield; stack empty; Lightning Bolt in graveyard; p2 at 37 life
actions.note: Reckless Bushwhacker entered the battlefield via its surge cost {1}{R} (vs normal {2}{R}). The engine should allow the surge alternative cost because Lightning Bolt was cast earlier this turn. Reckless Bushwhacker has haste per its oracle text.
